nodes.name: Telegram Webhook Trigger
nodes.notes:
STEP 1 — Telegram sends every user message here.
Set this URL in your Telegram bot webhook:
  POST https://api.telegram.org/bot<TOKEN>/setWebhook?url=<N8N_URL>/webhook/telegram-inbound

nodes.name: Extract & Route
nodes.notes:
STEP 2 — Parse the Telegram payload and decide which workflow to use.
Keyword routing example:
  'weather in Jakarta' → weather_report workflow
  'create ticket for...' → jira_triage workflow
  Everything else → general workflow

nodes.name: Call Agent API
nodes.notes:
STEP 3 — Send the message to the FastAPI agent.
The agent processes it through the full security pipeline
and returns a JSON response with the answer.

nodes.name: Send Telegram Reply
nodes.notes:
STEP 4 — Send the agent's answer back to the Telegram user.
The chat_id from step 2 tells Telegram which conversation to reply to.

nodes.name: Respond to Telegram
nodes.notes:
STEP 5 — Respond 200 OK to Telegram's webhook POST.
Telegram requires a fast 200 response or it will retry the message.
We respond immediately; the actual reply is sent in step 4.
